mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals expanding economic output, which increases corporate earnings and supports higher equity prices over the next 60 trading days. This is a slow-moving trend signal.
failure_condition: If rolling 20-day SPX returns are negative in more than 60% of periods when GDPC1 is above its 4-quarter moving average, or if rank IC over 60-day horizons is consistently below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.4 percentage points within two months signals imminent economic contraction, leading to reduced corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.4 percentage points within a 2-month period
failure_condition: If in at least 70% of triggered instances, SPX 20-day return is positive, or if the average return after triggers is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is elevated relative to inflation (real rate above 2%), the discount rate on equities increases, compressing valuations and causing negative forward returns within a week.
candidate_trigger: real FEDFUNDS rate (FEDFUNDS minus annual CPI inflation from CPIAUCSL) exceeds 2%
failure_condition: If average SPX 5-day return after trigger instances is positive, or if the conditional Sharpe ratio is higher than unconditional.

mechanism_narrative: During industrial recessions (INDPRO below 100), a rise in industrial production signals the start of a recovery, boosting investor confidence and leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns during periods of INDPRO < 100 and rising INDPRO are not positive on average (median negative or t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings and pushes equity prices higher over the short term.
failure_condition: If the rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month changes with forward 5-day SPX returns is less than 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.5%, it signals aggressive monetary easing, which supports asset prices through lower discount rates and stimulates equity demand.
failure_condition: If average SPX 20-day return after trigger is not positive (t-test p > 0.05), or if more than half of triggered periods show negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive monthly growth in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) confirms strong consumer sector momentum and broad economic health, leading to positive equity returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: both HOUST and RSAFS show positive month-over-month growth
failure_condition: If the average 20-day SPX return after simultaneous positive growth is not significantly positive, or if the signal fails to generate positive returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion with unemployment already very low (UNRATE below 4%), further increases in payrolls (PAYEMS) signal an overheating economy and rising wage pressures, which precede a market downturn.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward SPX return is positive in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS increases during UNRATE < 4%, or if the conditional return distribution is not significantly negative.
